reasoning: The OAS spread as of March 14, 2025 is 3.61. This is a noticeable local high compared to the 3.31-3.52 range seen between November 2024 and late February 2025. This recent widening was driven by a defensive risk-off move and tariff fears (an 8-day widening streak into early March). Because 3.61 is at the top of the recent 4-month range, the short-term probability (1-4 weeks) favors a downside mean-reversion back towards the 3.40-3.50 level as the initial market panic subsides, especially since JPM projects very low default rates (<1%) for EM corporate debt in 2025, and fundamentals remain structurally decent. Over longer horizons (6-12 months), while the spread is tight relative to its mid-2024 level (which suggests some upside risk), long-term uncertainty naturally draws the probabilities toward 0.5. Thus, the near-term probability of an increase is below 50%, slowly converging to 50% for the distant dates.
